title: WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM - 211info
text: HOMES FOR GOOD WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to income-eligible homeowners and renters. Older adults and adults with disabilities are prioritized. Job costs are paid by funding from federal grants and participating utilities. Energy audits and inspections are done by agency staff. Installation is completed by licensed and bonded private contractors. Weatherization measures designed to reduce energy consumption may include: -Attic, wall, and floor insulation -Improved venting in attic and underfloor -Whole house air sealing and installation of continuous ventilation -Weatherstripping of doors -Installation of thermal replacement windows -Installation of carbon monoxide detectors and smoke alarms -Sealing and insulation of HVAC ducts -Diagnostic testing to identify and locate air leakage -Servicing of furnaces and installation of digital thermostats -Minor weatherization-related health and safety repairs (no emergency home repairs) -Installation of energy-efficient refrigerators and compact fluorescent bulbs 100 W 13th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Income-qualified applicants (homeowners or renters). Certain households are given priority in accordance with federal guidelines, as well as available funding resources: 1st priority: Households with an older adult (age 60 or older) 2nd priority: Households with a member who receives Social Security Disability benefits 3rd priority: Households with children age 6 and under 4th priority: All other households Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 682-2561 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 682-2561 Español Energy (541) 682-3427 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish by arrangement AREA SERVED: Lane County Services: Weatherization Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - AGENCY-OWNED HOUSING HOUSING/SHELTER - PUBLIC HOUSING HOUSING/SHELTER - SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HOMES FOR GOOD
